content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Mrs. G. X. McKenna 
Post Office Box 1279 
Oceanside, CA 
92054 
Dear Mrs. McKenna: 
Julv 31, 1986 
RE: 
FPPC Advice No. A-86-225 
This is in response to your letter of July 1, 1986, and our 
telephone conversation of July 24, requesting clarification of 
the filing requirements of the Oceanside Taxpayers Association's 
political action committee. 
FACTS 
The Oceanside Taxpayers Association qualified as a recipient 
committee in 1984 and was required to file campaign disclosure 
statements. 
The committee filed statements covering the period 
January 1, 1984 through March 30, 1984. 
The committee did not 
file a semi-annual statement for the period March 31, 1984 
through June 30, 1984. 
The committee is currently receiving 
contributions and making expenditures. 
QUESTION 
You have asked what campaign disclosure statements the 
committee must file to be in compliance with the Political 
Reform Act. 
ANSWER 
Pursuant to Government Code Section 84200, the Oceanside 
Taxpayers Association's political action committee was required 
to file a semi-annual statement in 1984. 
The period covered by 
this statement is March 31, 1984 through June 30, 1984. 
As we 
discussed on the telephone, the treasurer should file Form 450, 
Recipient Committee Campaign Statement-Short Form as soon as 
possible and attach a cover letter explaining why the form is 
being filed late and request a waiver of any late fines. 
The 
committee is subject to a late fine of $10 per day until the 
late statement is filed with the city clerk's office. 
(Gov. 
Code section 91013) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660 


Mrs. G. X. McKenna 
Page Two 
It is my understanding that the committee treasurer resigned 
in 1984. 
At that time, the committee should have amended its 
statement of organization, Form 410, reporting the new 
treasurer's name. 
You indicated that you would contact the 
current treasurer and have her submit an amended Form 410 and 
attach a cover letter explaining why the amendment is late and 
request a waiver of any late fines. 
During the period July I, 1984 through December 31, 1985, 
the committee did not receive any contributions and did not make 
any expenditures and was therefore not required to file any 
campaign statements. 
Please note that beginning January I, 
1986, all recipient committees are required to file semi-annual 
campaign statements whether or not they have had any activity. 
For the first half of 1986, the committee must file a 
semi-annual statement reporting the contributions it has 
received and the expenditures it has made from January I, 1986 
through June 30, 1986. 
The period covered by this statement is 
the day after the closing date of the last campaign statement 
filed by the committee, which in this case is July I, 1984, 
through June 30, 1986. 
The statement is due July 31, 1986. 
To determine when the committee must file its next campaign 
disclosure statements, please refer to the "Information Manual 
on campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act," 
copy enclosed. 
I have enclosed the following materials which I would 
appreciate your forwarding on to the treasurer of this committee: 
• 
1986 Information Manual on campaign Disclosure 
Provisions of the Political Reform Act 
• 
1986 Record Keeping Manual 
• 
1986 Guide for Candidates and Treasurers 
• 
1986 Forms: 
410 - statement of Organization 
• 
1984 Form: 
405 -
Amendment to campaign Disclosure 
statement 
420 - Recipient Committee Campaign 
statement-Long Form 
450 - Recipient Committee Campaign 
statement-Short Form 
450 - Recipient Committee Campaign 
statement-Short Form 


Mrs. G. X. KcKenna 
Page Three 
• 
Political Reform Act of 1974 as Amended to 
January 1, 1986 
• 
FPPC Regulation 18427-Duties of Treasurers With Respect 
to Campaign statements 
• 
Copy of this letter 
I will be happy to assist the treasurer with any questions 
she may have in completing any of the forms mentioned above or 
with any other questions that may arise after reviewing the 
enclosed materials. 
The telephone number for the Technical 
Assistance and Analysis Division is (916) 322-5662. 
Feel free 
to call collect. 
MAK:ch 
Enclosures 
cc: 
Treasurer 
Sincerely, 
rJ'~~ 
Mary A~ Kvasager U 
Political Reform Consultant 
Oceanside Taxpayers Association 
Political Action Committee 


Oceanside Taxpayers Assqciation PAC 
7/1/86 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K St 
Sacramento Ca 95814 
Dear Sir: 
JUL II 3 41 PH ' 
This organization was in existance for a few years and then it was 
allowed to become dormant. The last papers filed on the PAC were 
about a year ago. 
I have reactivated this group and we have about 62 members who 
pay $15 per year for dues. 
From the reactivation to 6/15/86 no money was spent except for 
mailings to the members and no money was collected except for 
membership dues. 
I want to make sure that all I have to do is fill out a set of papers 
at the city clerk's office to bring this PAC current with the laws of 
the state and your commission. 
Please advise if there is anything that I should do at this point 
in time to get us into a "good standings" position. 
Any information will be appreciated as we are novices at PAC 
rules. 
Very truly, 
Mrs. G. X. McKenna 
PO Box 1279 
Oceanside Ca 92054 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
July 14, 1986 
Mrs. G. X. McKenna 
Oceanside Taxpayers Association PAC 
P.O. Box 1279 
Oceanside, CA 
92054 
Re: 
86-225 
Dear Mrs. McKenna: 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received on July 11, 1986 by the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and 
Analysis Division at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
JP:plh 
Very truly yours, 
i Jeanne Pritchard 
Chief 
Technical Assistance 
Division 
and Analysis 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 •
